text: This exercise is ATW or around the world needle driving. It is done on the foam simulated suture pad, which is also used in the Big Dipper needle drive exercise. This exercise requires you to suture from an outside to in fashion, and the needle throws will be done in a counterclockwise order.

The exercise begins at the twelve o'clock position on the foam simulated suture pad. The entry site is marked by a flashing gray dot, which turns green upon correct needle puncture. The exit site is similarly highlighted, confirming successful needle passage.

I found that this tissue is a little more forgiving in terms of excessive force and tissue penalties on the pull through compared to some of the other suture exercises. But it's still very important to make sure you follow the curve and the needle out on your pull through without any excessive force in order to avoid those penalties.

The surgeon alternates between the left and right needle driver based on the orientation of the needle throw. The needle is positioned and grabbed in a pronated position with the left hand to facilitate the next throw, demonstrating wrist articulation planning for efficient suture execution.

The surgeon uses the left needle driver to pull the suture through the tissue, following the curve of the needle. The needle is then aligned horizontally to be loaded onto the right-handed needle driver for the next throw.

You may have to take a couple practice runs at this and figure out the best coordination between the left and right arm with your loading and pull through in anticipation of your throws. And this will allow you to complete this exercise quickly and successfully.
